depth_universality_finding.interpretation: The 89% depth fraction hypothesis is not supported. Probe AUROC peaks span 33%–100% of network depth across 2 models. Fisher J peaks span 69%–79% of depth, which is closer to 89% but not universal. The discrepancy between Fisher peak (training signal) and probe peak (test performance) suggests the Fisher ratio is not a reliable indicator of where the best probe layer is. Revised claim: hallucination signals emerge in the second half of the network (>50% depth) for both models, but precise depth fraction is architecture-dependent.
depth_universality_finding.revised_claim: Hallucination signals emerge after 50% network depth; exact fraction is architecture-specific
